perturbation.original_text: As used in this letter agreement (this “Agreement”), the term “Evaluation Material” means all information regarding the Company, its businesses, technology, products, prospects and plans, a Transaction or the fact that the Company may pursue a Transaction with you or other parties, the existence and terms of this Letter Agreement and other information furnished to you in connection with the Transaction by the Company or any of its Representatives (as defined below), irrespective of form or medium of communication, whether prepared by the Company, its Representatives or otherwise and whether furnished before, on or after the date of this Agreement, together with any and all analyses, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other materials prepared by you or your Representatives that contain, are based on or otherwise incorporate, in whole or part, such information.
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this letter agreement (this “Agreement”), the term “Evaluation Material” means only written information explicitly labeled as 'Evaluation Material' regarding the Company’s financial projections, key personnel, and legal standings, furnished directly to you in connection with the Transaction by the Company's Director of Corporate Development, irrespective of the date when the information was shared. It excludes all analyses, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other materials prepared by you or your Representatives, unless approved in writing by the Company's CFO.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by narrowing the definition of 'Evaluation Material.' The original definition includes all information related to the company's businesses, technology, products, prospects, and plans. The modified definition restricts it to explicitly labeled written information regarding financial projections, key personnel, and legal standings, furnished by a specific person and excluding analyses done by the other part. This creates uncertainty because later sections discussing the use and return of 'Evaluation Material' now have conflicting interpretations. This directly contradicts the original broad scope of the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In consideration of, and as a condition to, your being furnished with such information and any other Evaluation Material (as defined below), the Company hereby requests your agreement as set forth herein. As used in this letter agreement (this “Agreement”), the term “Evaluation Material” means all information regarding the Company, its businesses, technology, products, prospects and plans, a Transaction or the fact that the Company may pursue a Transaction with you or other parties, the existence and terms of this Letter Agreement and other information furnished to you in connection with the Transaction by the Company or any of its Representatives (as defined below), irrespective of form or medium of communication, whether prepared by the Company, its Representatives or otherwise and whether furnished before, on or after the date of this Agreement, together with any and all analyses, compilations, summaries, forecasts, studies or other materials prepared by you or your Representatives that contain, are based on or otherwise incorporate, in whole or part, such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Evaluation Material' compared to the original definition and the contradicted text. The original and contradicted text include 'all information regarding the Company, its businesses, technology, products, prospects and plans'. The changed text restricts it to 'only written information explicitly labeled as 'Evaluation Material' regarding the Company’s financial projections, key personnel, and legal standings, furnished directly to you in connection with the Transaction by the Company's Director of Corporate Development'. This direct conflict in scope creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted text relies on the broader definition to apply to other clauses.

perturbation.original_text: You and your Representatives (i) will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you or your affiliates and (ii) will keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with the paragraph titled “Compelled Disclosure” below) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any information in the Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, will be provided with a copy of this Agreement and will agree to be bound by the terms hereof).
perturbation.changed_text: You and your Representatives (i) will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you or your affiliates and (ii) will keep the Evaluation Material confidential, unless prior written consent is acquired or implied. Your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, and this requirement can be waived with verbal agreement by the Company’s representative.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction concerning the confidentiality of the 'Evaluation Material.' The original text mandates strict confidentiality and prior written consent for any disclosure, only relaxed by 'Compelled Disclosure'. The modified text introduces the concepts of 'implied' consent and verbal waivers by a Company representative, creating ambiguity in how confidentiality is maintained. This change directly contradicts the earlier, stricter requirement for written consent, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: You and your Representatives (i) will use the Evaluation Material solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company involving you or your affiliates and (ii) will keep the Evaluation Material strictly confidential and (except as required by applicable law, regulation or legal process, and only after compliance with the paragraph titled “Compelled Disclosure” below) will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, disclose any information in the Evaluation Material to any person, except that the Evaluation Material (or portions thereof) may be disclosed to those of your Representatives who need to know such information solely for the purpose of evaluating a possible Transaction with the Company (it being understood that prior to such disclosure your Representatives will be informed of the confidential nature of the Evaluation Material, will be provided with a copy of this Agreement and will agree to be bound by the terms hereof).
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'implied' consent and verbal waivers for the confidentiality of the Evaluation Material, which directly contradicts the original and contradicted text that mandates 'strictly confidential' and 'prior written consent'. The changed text creates ambiguity and undermines the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Representatives (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto).
perturbation.changed_text: You agree to be responsible for any gross negligence or willful misconduct of this Agreement by your Representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause establishes that you are responsible for *any* breach of the agreement committed by your representatives. The change limits the responsibility to only 'gross negligence or willful misconduct'. This introduces a contradiction because it is unclear whether you are liable for all breaches or only those resulting from gross negligence or willful misconduct of your representatives. Normal breaches of the contract due to negligency or not, would be considered a breach to the agreement or not?
perturbation.contradicted_text: You agree to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by your Representatives (including, without limitation, any actions or inactions by your Representatives that would constitute a breach if such Representatives were original signatories hereto).
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the responsibility for breaches of the agreement by representatives to only 'gross negligence or willful misconduct', while the contradicted text states that you are responsible for 'any breach'. This directly contradicts the original, broader obligation, making it unclear whether you are liable for all breaches or only those resulting from gross negligence or willful misconduct.

perturbation.original_text: Non-Solicitation of Employees Until the earlier of (i) the consummation by you of a Transaction, (ii) eighteen months after termination of discussions relating to a possible Transaction between you and the Company, and (iii) two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for purposes of employment, offer to hire, entice away or offer to enter into any contract with, or hire any employee of the Company: provided, however, that this paragraph shall not prohibit you or any of your Representatives from engagement in any general advertising or general solicitation not targeted to the Company’s employees or hiring employees who respond to such a solicitation with no other action or by you or your Representatives in violation of this provision: and provided, further, that, following execution by you and the Company of a definitive agreement providing for a Transaction between you and the Company, this paragraph shall not prohibit you or any of your Representatives from offering employees employment in anticipation of and conditioned upon the consummation of such Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Solicitation of Employees Until the earlier of (i) the consummation by you of a Transaction, (ii) eighteen months after termination of discussions relating to a possible Transaction between you and the Company, and (iii) two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, solicit for purposes of employment, offer to hire, or hire any employee of the Company. However, you may hire any former employee of the Company if they have been terminated by the Company for over six months.
perturbation.explanation: The contradiction arises because the original text had some exceptions to Non-Solicitation of Employees rule. The change states that you won't solicit, offer or hire employees, but includes a new exception: you may hire former employees terminated for over six months. The original clause has specific exclusions for general advertising and hiring employees who respond to general solicitations. The contradictory_location is in the first paragraph that mentions you will not solicite any employee, but this new exception added to the clause, directly contradicts that clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Solicitation of Employees Until the earlier of (i) the consummation by you of a Transaction, (ii) eighteen months after termination of discussions relating to a possible Transaction between you and the Company, and (iii) two years from the date of this Agreement, you will not, without the Company’s prior written consent, directly or indirectly solicit for purposes of employment, offer to hire, entice away or offer to enter into any contract with, or hire any employee of the Company: provided, however, that this paragraph shall not prohibit you or any of your Representatives from engagement in any general advertising or general solicitation not targeted to the Company’s employees or hiring employees who respond to such a solicitation with no other action or by you or your Representatives in violation of this provision: and provided, further, that, following execution by you and the Company of a definitive agreement providing for a Transaction between you and the Company, this paragraph shall not prohibit you or any of your Representatives from offering employees employment in anticipation of and conditioned upon the consummation of such Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a new exception to the non-solicitation agreement, allowing the hiring of former employees terminated for over six months. This directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text, which states that the party will not solicit, offer to hire, or hire *any* employee of the company. While the original text has exceptions for general advertising, this new exception directly contradicts the blanket statement against hiring any employees, creating a clear in-text contradiction as defined by Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In addition, you hereby acknowledge that you are aware, and that you will advise your Representatives who receive any Evaluation Material, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non public information from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer (and options, warrants and rights relating thereto) or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person (including, without limitation, any of your Representatives) is likely to purchase or sell such securities. You hereby agree that neither you nor your Representatives will use or communicate the Evaluation Material in violation of these laws.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition, you hereby acknowledge that you are aware, and that you will advise your Representatives who receive any Evaluation Material, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non public information from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer (and options, warrants and rights relating thereto). You hereby agree that neither you nor your Representatives will use the Evaluation Material in violation of these laws, unless prior approved with legal counsel that has written a formal request to the Company’s legal counsel.
perturbation.explanation: The original section stated that you are aware of that you nor your Representatives will use or communicate the Evaluation Material in violation of these laws. The perturbation states that, if priorly approved by legal counsel, they may use the Evaluation Material to violate those laws. This creates a direct conflict. This contradictios are happening in page 4, when talking about Prohibiton of Trading
perturbation.contradicted_text: In addition, you hereby acknowledge that you are aware, and that you will advise your Representatives who receive any Evaluation Material, that the United States securities laws prohibit any person who has received from an issuer material, non public information from purchasing or selling securities of such issuer (and options, warrants and rights relating thereto) or from communicating such information to any other person under circumstances in which it is reasonably foreseeable that such person (including, without limitation, any of your Representatives) is likely to purchase or sell such securities. You hereby agree that neither you nor your Representatives will use or communicate the Evaluation Material in violation of these laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that neither the recipient nor their representatives would use or communicate the Evaluation Material in violation of securities laws. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing such use or communication if priorly approved by legal counsel, potentially contradicting the initial prohibition. The contradicted text reiterates the absolute prohibition, creating a direct conflict with the changed text's exception. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists because the changed text allows something that the contradicted text expressly forbids.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement embodies the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto and supersedes any and all prior agreements, arrangements and understandings relating to the matters provided for herein. This Agreement may not be amended or modified in any manner nor may any of its provisions be waived except by written amendment executed by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement embodies the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto and supersedes any and all prior agreements, arrangements and understandings relating to the matters provided for herein. This Agreement may be amended or modified in any manner, including written or oral agreements executed by any of the parties’ representatives.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction regarding how the agreement can be amended or modified. The original text states the agreement embodies the entire agreement and can only be amended by *written* amendment executed by the parties. The change says that the agreement can be amended by written or oral agreements executed by *any* of the parties’ representatives. This contradicts the written requirement in the first sentence, by adding the 'oral' possibility for the agreement to be modified.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement embodies the entire agreement and understanding of the parties hereto and supersedes any and all prior agreements, arrangements and understandings relating to the matters provided for herein. This Agreement may not be amended or modified in any manner nor may any of its provisions be waived except by written amendment executed by the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text and contradicted text state that the agreement can only be amended by written amendment executed by the parties. However, the changed text states that the agreement can be amended by written or oral agreements executed by any of the parties’ representatives. This contradicts the original provision and the contradicted text, which stipulates written amendments only.
